leaks_to_head.how: SessionStart hook. Runs `git init` + `git commit -m 'Initial commit'` on HEAD when Claude Code starts in a directory that is not yet a git repo. NOT converted to checkpoint-core.sh. Creates a real HEAD commit (albeit with semantic-ish message 'Initial commit').
leaks_to_head.blame_impact: Future `git blame` on every file in the initial commit will point to this autogenerated commit, not a human engineer.

leaks_to_head.how: Duplicate of ensure-git-repo.sh — SessionStart hook creating 'Initial commit' on HEAD. NOT covered by 7-file refactor.
leaks_to_head.blame_impact: Same as above. Also: TWO SessionStart hooks with identical behavior is a duplication smell (should consolidate).
leaks_to_head.note: The BA spec's 'Search pattern: git commit, Search scope: /root/.claude/hooks/**' and 'All occurrences' list in Contract B missed these two files entirely. The search was apparently filtered to auto-snapshot paths but 'git commit' literally occurs here and writes to HEAD.

leaks_to_head.how: `/clean` command instructs Claude to run `git add -A && git commit -m "checkpoint: Before aggressive cleanup on $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"` directly on HEAD as Step 10 'Safety Checkpoint'. Non-semantic commit message ('checkpoint: Before...') pollutes HEAD every time /clean is invoked.
leaks_to_head.blame_impact: Every /clean invocation creates a HEAD commit with message like 'checkpoint: Before aggressive cleanup on 2026-04-16'. Directly poisons `git blame`.

leaks_to_head.how: `/test` command Step 10 instructs Claude to run `git add -A && git commit -m "checkpoint: Before test execution on $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"` on HEAD. Non-semantic commit message.
leaks_to_head.blame_impact: Same pattern as /clean; every /test invocation poisons HEAD with a dated 'checkpoint:' commit.

leaks_to_head.how: `safe_add()` function runs `git add .` on the REAL index BEFORE calling write_checkpoint. On every fswatch cycle the user's real `.git/index` is polluted with auto-staged files. The subsequent write_checkpoint uses an ISOLATED temp index so the commit goes to refs/checkpoints/* correctly — but the REAL index remains polluted. Any subsequent user-driven `git commit` will sweep the auto-staged changes into the user's semantic commit unless the user manually resets.
leaks_to_head.blame_impact: Indirect but severe. User writes a hand-crafted semantic commit, but it silently contains every file the fswatch daemon auto-staged behind them. Violates the 'separation of auto-snapshot from HEAD' contract the whole refactor was built on.

new_findings_outside_7_files: ensure-git-repo.sh (NEW HEAD-leak, not in BA spec's 7 files)
session-git-init.sh (NEW HEAD-leak, not in BA spec's 7 files)
commands/clean.md (NEW HEAD-leak via orchestrator-executed bash, not in BA spec)
commands/test.md (NEW HEAD-leak via orchestrator-executed bash, not in BA spec)
git-fswatch.sh:safe_add (residual real-index pollution; lib uses temp index but safe_add still runs on real index)
audit_1_surface.systemd_and_cron_scan: No systemd service or cron job runs `git commit` in this environment. happy-claude-cleanup.service only does process cleanup, not git operations.
audit_1_surface.audit_1_verdict: FAIL — 5 commit-creation leak paths outside the 7 known files.

subagents_with_commit_authority.leak_severity: none — this is a user-invoked command creating semantic commits, exactly the allowed path.
none — only creates a commit if the user says yes.

subagents_with_commit_authority.type: AUTO-COMMIT as Step 10 'safety checkpoint' (no user prompt; runs as part of /clean pipeline)
subagents_with_commit_authority.leak_severity: critical — this is a non-semantic auto-commit baked into the /clean pipeline. Violates SaaS-grade bar directly.

subagents_with_commit_authority.type: AUTO-COMMIT as Step 10 'safety checkpoint' (no user prompt; runs as part of /test pipeline)

subagents_with_commit_authority.location: agents/ba.md, agents/qa.md, agents/dev.md, agents/pm.md, agents/cleaner.md, agents/test-executor.md
subagents_with_commit_authority.commit_style: N/A — grep confirms no subagent instructs itself to `git commit` outside user-approved semantic paths. agents/cleaner.md and agents/test-executor.md mention `checkpoint` only in context or reference field names.
semantic (merge-overnight creates real merge commits; dev-overnight delegates to dev subagent which uses semantic offers)
subagents_with_commit_authority.leak_severity: none detected — but note: dev-overnight writes auto-commits via the SessionStart hooks which WILL fire and pollute HEAD via session-git-init.sh if the overnight worktree is not already a git repo.

non_semantic_commit_sources.message_template: checkpoint: Before aggressive cleanup on YYYY-MM-DD
non_semantic_commit_sources.invocation: orchestrator executes as part of /clean pipeline, no gate

non_semantic_commit_sources.message_template: checkpoint: Before test execution on YYYY-MM-DD
non_semantic_commit_sources.invocation: orchestrator executes as part of /test pipeline, no gate

audit_2_subagents.audit_2_verdict: FAIL — /clean and /test commands emit non-semantic 'checkpoint:' commits directly to HEAD. These are orchestrator-executed, not user-approved.

forward_regression_check.only_new_commit: 16ed3fd chore(agents): harden BA/QA/dev workflows against 6-iteration failure pattern
forward_regression_check.verdict: PASS — No new non-semantic HEAD commits since the v2 refactor went live.

checkpoint_ref_activity_since_refactor.verdict: Refactor is WIRED and ACTIVE. All 68 post-refactor auto-snapshots live on refs/checkpoints/master, not master HEAD.

blame_samples.note: The new v2 library FILE ITSELF was introduced via a polluted 'checkpoint: Auto-save' commit. Every line of the fix blames to an Auto-save commit. This is the single most ironic finding of the audit.
Line 2 ('model: sonnet') blames to a checkpoint auto-save. Rest of the block blames to semantic commits.
Clean blame — all 10 lines point to semantic commits (d0a12cab 2026-03-23, original creation).

audit_3_production.out_of_scope_reference: BA spec v2 Won't-Have #1: 'Retroactive cleanup of existing checkpoint commits on master'. This is acknowledged. But note: iter2 changes to checkpoint-core.sh have ONLY landed on refs/checkpoints/master, never promoted to HEAD. So `git blame hooks/lib/checkpoint-core.sh` STILL shows the iter1 version (with the dangling-commit bug) as blamed to dafb2907. The iter2 flock fix is invisible to blame unless someone creates a semantic 'fix(checkpoints): add flock serialization' commit on master HEAD.
audit_3_production.audit_3_verdict: PARTIAL PASS — no NEW HEAD pollution since refactor (forward-goal met), but past pollution includes the v2 fix itself, making the library file a self-referential blame disaster. Recommend one follow-up semantic commit to promote the iter2 library content.

merge_in_progress.test: Created conflict via `git merge feat1` on a repo with competing changes. `.git/MERGE_HEAD` present. Called write_checkpoint.
merge_in_progress.result: HEAD did not move. MERGE_HEAD preserved. Checkpoint ref tip de21fd9 created containing the conflict-marker content of a.txt. No crash.
merge_in_progress.caveat: The checkpoint captures the conflict-marker state as the tree — this is technically correct for 'snapshot the current working tree' but means the user will see a checkpoint containing literal '<<<<<<<' markers. Acceptable, document in CLAUDE.md.

rebase_in_progress.test: Started `git rebase -i HEAD~3 -x 'false'` to force a pause. `.git/rebase-merge` present. Called write_checkpoint.
rebase_in_progress.result: HEAD did not move. Rebase state preserved. Library returned rc=0 but created no ref (detached HEAD during rebase → detached-<sha> ref path, which was empty in my run — subtle behavior).

cherry_pick_in_progress.test: Forced conflict via cherry-pick. `.git/CHERRY_PICK_HEAD` present. Called write_checkpoint.
cherry_pick_in_progress.result: HEAD did not move. CHERRY_PICK_HEAD preserved. Checkpoint written to ref. No crash.

corrupt_lockfile.result: rc=0, checkpoint created on ref. BUT: the library still `touch`es the lockfile unconditionally at line 293 BEFORE the permission-set test happens, which in my setup recreated or preserved a mode-644 lockfile. On a truly readonly git-dir (not tested), the `touch` would fail silently (`|| true`) and `9>"$lockfile"` would fail, causing the subshell redirect to fail and the flock block to abort with nonzero rc, falling through to the CAS-retry fallback path.
corrupt_lockfile.concern: Readonly GIT_DIR is an unusual but possible deployment. Lib would degrade from 'flock-safe' to 'CAS-retry with potential orphans'. Document or test explicitly. Minor.

parallel_sessions_same_repo.test: Spawned 2 concurrent bash subshells each sourcing the lib and calling write_checkpoint on the same ref.
parallel_sessions_same_repo.result: Both succeeded, 0 dangling commits, 2 linear commits on the ref. flock serialization handles this correctly.

ac6_20_writer_stress.result: 20 parallel workers with distinct files → 0 dangling commits (iter1 had 9 with 10 workers). Iter2 flock fix works as described.

nfs_flock.test: df -T shows /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/.git is on tmpfs, not NFS. Local tmpfs supports flock natively.
nfs_flock.concern_for_future: If the dot-claude tree were ever moved to an NFS share, `flock` semantics on NFS are version-dependent (pre-NFSv4 did not reliably honor advisory locks across clients). The lib would silently degrade to CAS-retry-fallback on such setups. The library does not detect this. Document as known limitation in CLAUDE.md.

live_repo_dangling_commits.note: Live repo already carries 7 dangling commits (from iter1 testing, before the flock fix). These are GC-eligible (default 2-week grace) but could be cleaned now with `git reflog expire --expire=now --all && git gc --prune=now`.

commit_graph_corruption.note: `git fsck` on the live /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude repo reports 20 'failed to parse commit ... from object database for commit-graph' errors. `.git/objects/info/commit-graph` references commits that no longer exist in the object DB. This is a real corruption, not a warning. Any tool that reads the commit-graph (git log, blame under certain conditions) may behave erratically. Fix: `git commit-graph write --reachable --progress` or `rm .git/objects/info/commit-graph` to force regeneration.
commit_graph_corruption.verdict_impact: This is NOT caused by the v2 refactor directly, but was likely seeded by older pre-refactor auto-commits that were rebased/squashed away. Regardless, a SaaS-grade repo should not have a broken commit-graph. Fix required.

audit_4_edge_cases.audit_4_verdict: PASS on the designed edge cases (merge, rebase, cherry-pick, parallel). FAIL on the LIVE repo hygiene: broken commit-graph + 7 dangling commits from iter1 were left in place after the fix landed.

log_rotation.finding: No logrotate config exists for ~/.claude/logs/checkpoint.log or checkpoint-push.log. /etc/logrotate.d/ has no 'claude' or 'checkpoint' entry. Current sizes: checkpoint.log 12.8KB (58 CAS-race warnings logged), checkpoint-push.log 2.7KB. Growth rate observable; unbounded over months.
log_rotation.recommendation: Add /etc/logrotate.d/claude-checkpoint with weekly rotation, 4-week retention, compress, postrotate hook to no-op (scripts always append).

monitoring.finding: No alerting on silent checkpoint failures. Callers do `if write_checkpoint; then echo ok; else echo failed; fi` — but the 'failed' message goes to stdout/stderr of the Stop hook and is LOST unless a human is watching. `_checkpoint_log ERROR` writes to the log file, but nothing reads the log. A SaaS-grade setup would need: (a) error-rate alert on checkpoint.log via logwatch or pushover, (b) daily sanity-check cron running `git fsck --no-dangling` on the repo and alerting if it fails.
monitoring.recommendation: Add a daily cron job that tails the last 24h of checkpoint.log, counts ERROR/WARN lines, and posts to a notification channel if above threshold.

ref_growth.finding: No pruning or retention policy for refs/checkpoints/*. refs/checkpoints/master currently has 68 commits (~2 weeks of activity). At current rate (~4-5 checkpoints/hour during active sessions) the ref will accumulate thousands of commits per month with no pruning. Over a year the ref history on a heavily-used branch will exceed the branch's own history by 100x.
ref_growth.recommendation: Add a scheduled pruner: keep only the last 200 checkpoints per ref, OR convert the ref to a reflog-style rolling entry, OR use `git for-each-ref --sort=committerdate` + `git update-ref -d` to delete old entries. Document retention policy in CLAUDE.md.

refspec_install_friction.finding: BA spec Won't-Have #2 explicitly excludes auto-modifying .git/config. Doc in CLAUDE.md:91 shows the fetch refspec for cross-machine recovery, but installation is a manual text-edit of .git/config. No install script provided. For SaaS onboarding, a new engineer cloning the repo gets ZERO checkpoint visibility until they manually edit their config. Friction is significant.
refspec_install_friction.recommendation: Add `hooks/install-checkpoint-refspec.sh` — one-line invocation that adds the fetch refspec to the user's origin remote. Keep it explicit (not auto-run on SessionStart) but document in CLAUDE.md.

parallel_session_safety.finding: Confirmed by test: two bash subshells calling write_checkpoint on the same repo+ref serialize correctly via the per-repo flock. No orphans.

audit_5_ops_hygiene.audit_5_verdict: FAIL — 4 of 5 ops concerns are unaddressed: log rotation, monitoring, ref growth, refspec install friction. These are all explicit 'Won't-Have' items in the BA spec, documented as future work, but the user's SaaS-grade bar calls for them to be addressed NOW.

verdict_summary: v2 refactor achieves FORWARD goal on master (no new HEAD pollution since 06:48:01) but MISSES 5 additional commit-creation surfaces (ensure-git-repo.sh, session-git-init.sh, commands/clean.md, commands/test.md, git-fswatch.sh safe_add), leaves the LIVE repo with a broken commit-graph + 7 dangling objects + unbounded checkpoint-log growth, and ships with no log rotation / no monitoring / no ref pruning / no refspec installer. Past pollution is out-of-scope per spec, but includes the v2 library file itself, so `git blame hooks/lib/checkpoint-core.sh` shows every line pointing to a 'checkpoint: Auto-save' commit — the most ironic SaaS-grade failure possible.

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: commands/clean.md:557-558 auto-commits non-semantic 'checkpoint: Before aggressive cleanup...' to HEAD as Step 10 of /clean. Every /clean invocation pollutes master blame.
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Replace `git add -A && git commit -m "checkpoint: Before aggressive cleanup..."` with `bash ~/.claude/hooks/checkpoint.sh "Before aggressive cleanup"` which routes through checkpoint-core.sh to refs/checkpoints/*.

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: hooks/git-fswatch.sh:274 safe_add() stages to the REAL index before every checkpoint. This silently pollutes the user's staging area and contaminates their next semantic commit.
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Delete the safe_add() function entirely. write_checkpoint already uses an isolated temp index via GIT_INDEX_FILE; the real index must never be touched by the fswatch daemon. Update sync_changes() to skip Step 2 'Add'.

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: hooks/ensure-git-repo.sh:81 and hooks/session-git-init.sh:81 both run `git commit -m 'Initial commit'` on HEAD as SessionStart hooks. For a new repo, the Initial commit pollutes blame with a Claude-attributed commit rather than a human engineer.
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Either (a) remove the auto-commit and instruct the user to make the initial commit manually, or (b) keep the initial commit but change its attribution to the current user (git config user.name) and make the body semantic-grade ('chore: initialize repository with default .gitignore'). Also: consolidate the two duplicate hooks into one.

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: Live /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/.git has 20 commit-graph errors and 7 dangling commits from iter1 testing.
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Run `rm .git/objects/info/commit-graph && git commit-graph write --reachable` to regenerate. Run `git reflog expire --expire=now --all && git gc --prune=now` to GC the iter1 orphans.

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: No log rotation for ~/.claude/logs/checkpoint.log and checkpoint-push.log. Will grow unbounded.
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Create /etc/logrotate.d/claude-checkpoint with weekly rotation, 4-week retention, compress, copytruncate.

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: No monitoring on checkpoint failures. Silent errors accumulate in log with no alerting.
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Add a daily cron or systemd timer that scans checkpoint.log for ERROR lines in the last 24h and surfaces them (desktop notification, pushover, email, whatever the user uses).

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: No pruning policy for refs/checkpoints/*. Ref growth is unbounded (68 commits in ~2 weeks of activity on a single branch).
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Add a weekly cron that trims each refs/checkpoints/<branch> to the most recent N (e.g., 200) commits using `git update-ref` + a rewritten chain. OR: switch to a reflog-style single-entry ref with rotation.

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: hooks/lib/checkpoint-core.sh itself was introduced on master via a 'checkpoint: Auto-save' commit (dafb2907). All 10 lines of the library blame to a non-semantic commit. Past pollution is out-of-scope, but the irony blocks the SaaS-grade bar for THIS specific file.
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Create a single semantic commit on master: `refactor(checkpoints): introduce checkpoint-core.sh v2 library` that promotes the current iter2 library content. This will remove the self-referential blame pollution on the refactor's own files.

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: Fetch refspec for cross-machine checkpoint recovery requires manual .git/config edit. New engineers onboarding cannot see checkpoints from other machines without friction.
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Add hooks/install-checkpoint-refspec.sh (one-line: `git config --add remote.origin.fetch '+refs/checkpoints/*:refs/remotes/origin/checkpoints/*'`). Document in CLAUDE.md.

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: NFS flock semantics not documented. If the dot-claude tree is ever moved to NFS, the lib silently degrades to CAS-retry-fallback (orphan commits possible).
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Add detection + explicit warning in the lib: test flock with a 1ms holder and warn if the lock is not honored. OR just document in CLAUDE.md 'this lib assumes local filesystem; on NFS the fallback CAS path may produce orphans'.

blockers_to_saas_grade.issue: Corrupt lockfile (mode 000, readonly GIT_DIR) would silently downgrade to CAS-fallback. Not tested in BA's AC list.
blockers_to_saas_grade.fix: Document or add an explicit test. Low priority because readonly GIT_DIR is exotic.

evidence_types: scratch-repo test runs with exit codes
self_verification.notes: Every finding backed by a direct command or file read. No claim is 'code looks correct'. The critical findings (ensure-git-repo.sh, session-git-init.sh, commands/clean.md:557, commands/test.md:372, git-fswatch.sh safe_add) were all confirmed by reading the actual file content, not just greping.
